steps.prompt: Which word is the main finite verb?

hints.transire: 'Transire' is an infinitive meaning 'to pass through' — it is the subject of 'est'
hints.camelum: 'Camelum' is a noun meaning 'camel' in the accusative
hints.Facilius: 'Facilius' is a comparative adverb meaning 'more easily'

hints.to eat: 'To eat' is 'edere' — 'est' here is from 'esse' (to be)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sit' — 'est' is indicative

hints.edo, esse, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat)
hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go)
hints.fio, fieri, factus sum, —: These are the parts of 'fieri' (to become)

hints.imperative: Imperative would be 'transi' — 'transire' is the infinitive
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'transeat' — 'transire' is infinitive
hints.gerund: A gerund would be 'transeundi' — 'transire' is the infinitive used as subject

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'camelus' — 'camelum' with -um is accusative
'Foramen' could be nominative, but after 'per' it must be accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'foramine' — 'foramen' after 'per' is accusative
hints.dative: Dative would be 'foramini' — 'foramen' is accusative after 'per'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'foraminis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'foraminis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'foraminis' ends in -is

hints.nominative: Nominative is also 'acus' (4th decl.), but here it modifies 'foramen' — genitive
hints.dative: Dative singular of 4th declension would be 'acui' — 'acus' is genitive
hints.accusative: Accusative singular would be 'acum' — 'acus' with -ūs is genitive (4th decl.)

hints.positive degree adjective: Positive degree would be 'facile' — 'facilius' has the comparative -ius ending
hints.noun in the nominative: 'Facilius' is not a noun — it is the comparative form of the adverb 'facile'

translation: It is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle
